lesson.menu_title: Retail & Fashion Tech Vocabulary — Presenting a Project
lesson.menu_desc: The words behind retail technology — and how to present a tech project to stakeholders.
lesson.subtitle: Aula 12 — Retail & Fashion Tech Vocabulary: Presenting a Project
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Daniela Feitoza | Aula 12 -- Retail & Fashion Tech Vocabulary

listenings.text: Hi Daniela, it is Sarah, your English coach. Today, listen to a short product walkthrough, the kind you might give to stakeholders. Welcome to the demo of our new retail platform. First, the customer opens the mobile app and browses the catalog. The app connects to our inventory system in real time, so the stock count is always correct. At the checkout, the payment gateway processes the card in two seconds. Behind the scenes, the dashboard shows live sales data to every store manager. Finally, our analytics engine studies the data and gives smart recommendations. Notice how I describe each part with a clear noun: the mobile app, the inventory system, the payment gateway, the analytics engine. These are the building blocks of any retail tech project. Try to describe your own system the same way. Talk soon.

Hello Daniela, this is Marcus from the vendor team. I want to give you a quick pitch for our new solution, so you can hear retail tech vocabulary in action. Our platform is fully scalable, which means it grows with your business. The user interface is simple and clean, so your store staff need very little training. We integrate with your existing inventory system and your point-of-sale terminals, so there is no painful migration. Our cloud-based dashboard gives you real-time analytics, from stock levels to customer behavior. And the whole rollout takes about six weeks. I know you manage technology for a large group, so reliability matters. Our uptime is ninety-nine point nine percent. Let us schedule a demo, and I will walk your stakeholders through every feature. Looking forward to it.
